author: role.analyst.dual-form-adventure

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player moves from capable ranch hand to heroic swordsman and sensory wolf, protecting communities while learning to cooperate with a morally complex guide.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: Enter a threatened province, investigate in the form currently permitted, restore access or light, pursue a dungeon and its tool, then revisit the connected field with new traversal and combat options.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Lock-on sword techniques, shield, aimed tools, climbing, swimming, riding, wolf scent vision, digging, howling, pack attacks, warping, and companion-assisted leaps provide two overlapping controllers.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Light and twilight conditions, restored provinces, portals, cleared hazards, opened shortcuts, and eventually freer human-wolf transformation alter which actors and routes are accessible.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: Large provincial fields connect settlements, caves, ruins, and dungeons; landmarks and unlocked warp points compress later travel across the expanded 3D scale.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Form, senses, scent trails, tools, mounted access, companion actions, story state, and warp portals gate routes; old fields expose hidden spaces after each capability increase.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Distinct surfaces, scent traces, shadow-only phenomena, companion prompts, tool targets, and mechanical animation tell the player which form or capability can change a state.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Large themed dungeons use keys, central spaces, bespoke mechanisms, new tools, shortcuts, and bosses, often emphasizing physical scale and a singular architectural identity.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Hidden caves, insects, spirits, health pieces, equipment upgrades, wallets, ammunition capacity, and optional combat instruction reward revisiting broad field routes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Health, wallets, quivers, bomb bags, bottles, armor and tools persist; rupees, arrows, bombs, oil-like supplies, and shield state regulate field and dungeon preparation.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Target-relative sword combat expands through learned techniques, mounted encounters, wolf attacks, and large bosses that expose positional or tool-mediated vulnerability in staged phases.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Settlements, rescue sequences, escorts, trading and collection tasks, and companion exchanges respond strongly to story restoration, though fewer characters run dense systemic schedules.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: Long-form cinematics, transformations, companion conflict, mounted set pieces, province restorations, and returns to threatened places foreground dramatic staging more than freeform chronology.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: Lock-on, sense overlays, mounted follow cameras, wide boss framing, reveal shots, and carefully blocked cutscenes maintain clarity across two body scales and large environments.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: Opening village work teaches context action, riding, aiming, animal handling, and social stakes; form-specific prompts, a companion, visual traces, and optional combat lessons extend instruction.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The original Wii and GameCube releases map the same core states differently: analog targeting remains central, while Wii adds pointer aiming and motion gestures to selected actions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Saving, fairies, bottles, health drops, dungeon shortcuts, unlocked warp points, and companion prompts preserve macro progress; the original provides limited formal assist modes.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: Realistic-scale 3D supports horseback speed, long approaches, vertical architecture, scent-following overlays, and large cinematic actors but increases camera, streaming, and return-travel costs.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The work is single-player and launched on Wii and GameCube; mirrored world orientation and different aiming or attack mappings are version-specific control expressions.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: A faithful abstraction needs paired human-wolf locomotion and interaction sets, target camera, trace visualization, mounted traversal, province variants, portals, cinematic state handoff, and dual input profiles.

observationThemes.context: A missing route, person, or hidden phenomenon leaves clues that ordinary human interaction cannot resolve.
preconditions: Wolf form is active or available.
Sense, scent, digging, or companion actions are relevant to the location.
playerInput: Change form when allowed, activate sensory vision, follow a trace, dig, howl, or use a form-specific interaction.
before: The environment appears incomplete or gives no ordinary target.
action: Adopt the alternate body's perceptual and movement vocabulary.
after: A trace, hidden actor, buried route, special leap, or other actionable relation becomes available.
feedback: Transformation staging, altered HUD prompts, sense overlay, scent visualization, animation, and companion response expose the new state.
consequences: Investigation becomes a player-controlled change of perceptual model rather than a passive clue reveal.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Wolf form can use senses to perceive otherwise hidden phenomena and follow scents, dig at selected ground, howl, and perform companion-assisted leaps.
observationThemes.interpretation: An alternate form is strongest when it changes what counts as evidence, not only movement speed or combat damage.

observationThemes.context: The player first traverses a province under an abnormal state and later resolves the region-scale objective.
preconditions: The required investigation or collection chain is complete.
playerInput: Finish the restoration transaction and re-enter the region afterward.
before: Lighting, occupants, form constraints, hazards, and route permissions express the abnormal state.
action: Change the province's governing story and environment flag.
after: Normal inhabitants and services return, routes become usable in a new form, and the next dungeon or field goal becomes legible.
feedback: Large visual transition, music, character reappearance, changed body state, and renewed interactions dramatize restoration.
consequences: The player experiences progress as a place becoming socially and spatially habitable, not only as a quest marker increment.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Early provinces are encountered under twilight and are later restored, changing lighting, accessible inhabitants, and how the hero can move and interact there.
observationThemes.interpretation: A region-scale before-and-after presentation can make a systemic flag carry emotional and navigational payoff.

observationThemes.context: A large dungeon presents architecture whose moving parts or distances exceed the hero's current reach.
preconditions: Targets and mechanisms are visually distinct.
playerInput: Aim or deploy the tool on a signaled target, then combine it with route navigation and earlier rooms.
before: A vertical gap, massive mechanism, distant anchor, or protected route blocks the dependency graph.
action: Use the new physical relation introduced by the tool.
after: Architecture moves, the hero crosses space differently, or a shortcut and reward become reachable.
feedback: Camera scale, mechanical animation, target response, sound, and opened geometry show both force and direction.
consequences: The acquisition changes the player's relationship to dungeon scale and later exposes optional field rewards.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Major dungeons award tools that repeatedly manipulate or traverse their large mechanisms and are subsequently usable in overworld secrets and combat.
observationThemes.interpretation: A tool feels substantial when level architecture visibly demonstrates a new physical relationship rather than presenting isolated locks.

observationThemes.context: A vulnerable character or vehicle must traverse a dangerous field while the hero rides and responds to threats.
preconditions: The escort route and mounted controller are active.
Threats can alter the protected actor's progress.
playerInput: Ride alongside, target attackers or hazards, manage position, and restore the route when disrupted.
before: Narrative urgency exists but the destination has not been reached.
action: Protect moving actors through a staged spatial sequence.
after: The escort advances, diverts, stalls, or reaches safety according to encounter state.
feedback: Follow camera, actor blocking, route motion, attack cues, damage reaction, and destination framing expose the shared risk.
consequences: Character stakes remain embodied in controllable movement instead of becoming a noninteractive cutscene.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The adventure includes mounted protection sequences in which moving allies and threats share the field and player actions determine whether the route continues.
observationThemes.interpretation: An escort can function as performance when protected actors visibly occupy the same causal space and recovery is staged rather than instant failure.

observationThemes.context: The player discovers an optional combat lesson and later meets enemies whose guard or attack pattern rewards that technique.
preconditions: The lesson has been found and completed.
The technique's positional or timing condition is present.
playerInput: Recognize the tell, execute the learned command, and follow through during the opening.
before: Ordinary attacks are inefficient or unsafe.
action: Use the optional advanced response against a compatible enemy state.
after: The hero repositions, breaks defense, or creates a safer damage window.
feedback: Dedicated lesson staging, move animation, enemy reaction, target camera, and retained move list confirm mastery.
consequences: Exploration yields expressive combat competence without making the main route depend on every optional lesson.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Optional encounters teach durable sword techniques that can later exploit particular enemy positions or attack states, while ordinary combat remains available.
observationThemes.interpretation: Optional teaching can reward curiosity with a more legible recovery option rather than a mandatory numerical upgrade.

contributionHypotheses: An alternate body can make investigation systemic by changing visible evidence, traversal, and social access together.
A large regional state transition can fuse narrative catharsis with new navigation and service availability.
Optional authored lessons can turn exploration rewards into new responses to existing combat tells.
uniqueOrNonportable: The exact light-versus-twilight cosmology, named companion, wolf transformation design, characters, provinces, artifacts, and plot incidents are recognizable authored surface content and must not be reused.
Specific scent trails, restoration sequences, dungeon architecture, boss fights, mounted set pieces, sword lessons, and cinematic compositions are nonportable.
Wii mirroring, gesture mappings, and the GameCube control layout are version-specific historical expressions rather than portable design rules.
openQuestions: Wii and GameCube original releases should receive separate control and orientation delta traces before input evidence is aggregated.
A later route simulation should distinguish mandatory wolf investigation from optional sense-based discovery across the full work.
